exercises.code:
import unicodedata
names = ['José', 'Jose\u0301', '']
for s in names:
    print(repr(s), '→', repr(unicodedata.normalize('NFC', s)))

a, b = 'MAÑANA', 'mañana'
match = a.casefold() == b.casefold()
print(match)

import unicodedata
a = 'José'
b = 'Jose\u0301'
print('raw equal', a == b)
na, nb = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', a), unicodedata.normalize('NFC', b)
print('NFC equal', na == nb)
print('causa: NFD residual (combining mark) vs NFC compuesto')

raw = 'Ana María Quispe Huamán'
toks = raw.split()
ap1, ap2 = toks[-2:]
given = ' '.join(toks[:-2])
print(given)
print(ap1, ap2)

raw = 'María del Carmen Quispe Ríos'
toks = raw.split()
ap1, ap2 = toks[-2:]
given = ' '.join(toks[:-2])
print(given)
print(ap1, ap2)

exercises.code:
line = 'C001, Ana , Lima'
fields = [p.strip() for p in line.split(',')]
print(fields)

toks = ['Jr.', 'Unión', '450']
print(' '.join(toks))
print('-'.join(toks))

raw = '999.000-111'
digits = ''.join(ch for ch in raw if ch.isdigit())
print(digits)

raw = '(+51) 999-000-111'
digits = ''.join(ch for ch in raw if ch.isdigit())
print(digits)

import re
email = 'user+tag@example.com'
pat = r'^[a-z]+@[a-z]+\.com$'
rejected = re.fullmatch(pat, email) is None
print('rejected', rejected)
print('Política: preferir strip+lower y presencia de @; no overfit de regex')

import re
pat = r'^[A-Z]{3}$'
print(bool(re.fullmatch(pat, 'LIM')))
print(bool(re.fullmatch(pat, 'Lima')))

import re
pat = re.compile(r'^(?P<nom>\w+) (?P<ap>\w+)$')
m = pat.fullmatch('Ana Quispe')
print(m.groupdict() if m else None)

import re
text = 'DNI 12345678'
print('search', bool(re.search(r'\d{8}', text)))
print('fullmatch', bool(re.fullmatch(r'\d{8}', text)))
print('Nota: fullmatch exige la cadena entera; search encuentra en medio')

import re
phone = re.compile(r'\b9\d{8}\b')
for s in ['ok 999111222', 'solo 12345']:
    print(s, phone.findall(s))

import re
log = 'ok LIM-01 y CUS-02 fin'
codes = re.findall(r'[A-Z]{3}-\d{2}', log)
print(codes)

print("patrón peligroso: (a+)+b sobre strings largos de a's")
print('Riesgo: catastrophic backtracking con cuantificadores anidados ambiguos')
print('Mitigación: preferir a+b o métodos str; patrones simples')

evidence = {
    'raw_a': 'Juan Perez',
    'raw_b': 'Juan P Perez',
    'score': 0.67,
    'decision': 'review',
    'reason': 'Jaccard medio; no auto-fusionar',
}
print(evidence)

print('1) Jaccard no es fuente autoritativa de identidad')
print('2) Un score no prueba parentesco ni vínculo legal')
print('3) Riesgo ético/producto: score → revisión humana con evidencia, no etiqueta familiar')
